input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-09T00:58:57.474Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to declare light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current theme and picks the matching value. It **reacts automatically** when the user toggles the system theme — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme<T>()` or `SetAppThemeColor()`:

```csharp
// For Color properties (shorthand)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any property type
myButton.SetAppTheme<Brush>(Button.BackgroundProperty,
    light: new SolidColorBrush(Color.FromArgb("#6200EE")),
    dark:  new SolidColorBrush(Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC")));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Triggers** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Reactivity** | Updates live — no restart or page reload required |
| **Scope** | Works on any bindable property, not just colors (e.g., `ImageSource`, `FontSize`) |
| **Override theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to force a mode |
| **Best practice** | Extract repeated values into resource dictionaries with `AppThemeBinding` for consistency |

**Resource dictionary pattern** (scales better):

```xml
<!-- In App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}</Color>
</Application.Resources>

<!-- In any page -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}">
    <Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryText}" />
</ContentPage>
```

This keeps all theme values in one place rather than scattered across pages.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a tutorial/explanation on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to support light/dark mode theming. This is a knowledge question — they want a code example and explanation, not changes to an existing codebase. I'll provide a concise, practical answer.
